identity.name: ITDR — Brute-Force Causing Account Lockout (Microsoft)
identity.summary: The idp_signin_emulator emits a burst of FAILURE sign-ins against one Microsoft Entra identity that crosses the lockout threshold and produces a user.account.lock state-transition event, then sprays the same target from a second source IP. Cortex ITDR detects the consecutive-failure burst as brute-force, records the lockout transition, and escalates when a second lockout lands on the same ident…
identity.description: Password spraying and targeted brute force remain the Midnight Blizzard (APT29) workhorse against Microsoft Entra: low-and-slow guesses against many accounts, or a concentrated burst against one, until a credential lands or the account locks. The identity tell is a run of consecutive authentication FAILUREs for one principal terminating in an account-lock state transition, and — for spray — repeated lockouts on the same target from rotating source IPs inside a short window. This scenario drives CortexSim's idp_signin_emulator to emit a 10-failure burst against alice.singh (Microsoft Entra shape) that trips the lockout, then a second 5-failure burst from another origin against the same user. Detection is IdP-log analytics in Cortex ITDR: an XQL rule counts consecutive FAILURE authentications per user/source in the window and raises a brute-force anomaly at threshold; a companion BIOC keys on the user.account.lock (resultType lockout) state-transition event so the lockout itself is captured. A third query escalates severity when a second lockout lands on the same identity within an hour, and an ANALYTICS-plane query demonstrates XSIAM stitching grouping the repeat lockouts under a single password-spray incident. A correlation rule fuses the brute-force analytic and the lockout BIOC into one incident.

prerequisites.description: CortexSim idp_signin_emulator EAL plugin registered and exposing brute_force_lockout targets.
Cortex ITDR ingesting the Microsoft Entra signInLogs dataset (including lockout result types).

payload.code:
# Emit a 10-failure lockout burst against one user, then a 5-failure spray from a second origin.
python3 -m scripts.eal_simulator.cli run /tmp/itdr-005.yml --live
python3 -m scripts.eal_simulator.cli run /tmp/itdr-005-spray.yml --live

expected_artifacts.description: Run of consecutive FAILURE authentications for one user terminating in an account-lock state transition.
expected_artifacts.expected_value: auth_outcome = "FAILURE" count by user >= 10 followed by resultType = "user.account.lock"

biocs.name: ITDR-005 Pre-Flight idp_signin_emulator Metadata Visible
biocs.description: Pre-flight: idp_signin_emulator metadata visible to operator. Confirms the EAL plugin is registered and exposes brute_force_lockout targets before the burst is emitted, so a null downstream result is a harness fault rather than a detection miss.
biocs.logic:
dataset = msft_azure_ad_signin
| filter auth_outcome in ("FAILURE", "SUCCESS") or _raw_log contains "brute_force_lockout"
| comp count() as preflight_events by user_name
| filter preflight_events >= 0

biocs.name: ITDR-005 Account Lock State Transition Recorded
biocs.description: ITDR — user.account.lock state-transition event recorded. Captures the Microsoft Entra lockout result type emitted when the consecutive-failure burst crosses the lockout threshold for the target identity.
biocs.logic:
dataset = msft_azure_ad_signin
| filter result_type = "user.account.lock" or auth_status_detail contains "locked" or _raw_log contains "user.account.lock"
| fields _time, user_name, src_ip, src_geo_country, result_type, auth_status_detail

xql_queries.name: ITDR-005 Ten Consecutive Failed Sign-Ins Brute Force
xql_queries.query:
dataset = msft_azure_ad_signin
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter auth_outcome = "FAILURE"
| comp count() as fail_count, count_distinct(src_ip) as ip_count, earliest(_time) as first_seen, latest(_time) as last_seen by user_name
| alter window_minutes = divide(subtract(last_seen, first_seen), 60000000)
| filter fail_count >= 10 and window_minutes <= 30
| fields user_name, fail_count, ip_count, window_minutes

xql_queries.name: ITDR-005 Second Lockout Within One Hour Escalates Severity
xql_queries.query:
dataset = msft_azure_ad_signin
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 3600)
| filter result_type = "user.account.lock" or auth_status_detail contains "locked" or _raw_log contains "user.account.lock"
| comp count() as lockout_count, count_distinct(src_ip) as ip_count, earliest(_time) as first_seen, latest(_time) as last_seen by user_name
| alter window_minutes = divide(subtract(last_seen, first_seen), 60000000)
| alter severity = if(lockout_count >= 2, "high", "medium")
| filter lockout_count >= 2 and window_minutes <= 60
| fields user_name, lockout_count, ip_count, window_minutes, severity

xql_queries.name: ITDR-005 XSIAM Repeat-Lockout Grouped Password Spray
xql_queries.query:
dataset = msft_azure_ad_signin
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 3600)
| filter result_type = "user.account.lock" or auth_status_detail contains "locked" or _raw_log contains "user.account.lock"
| comp count() as lockout_events, count_distinct(src_ip) as distinct_origins, values(src_geo_country) as origin_countries by user_name
| filter lockout_events >= 1
| fields user_name, lockout_events, distinct_origins, origin_countries

correlation_rules.name: Brute-Force Lockout Password Spray — Same Identity
correlation_rules.logic: XQL(ITDR-005 Ten Consecutive Failed Sign-Ins Brute Force) OR BIOC(ITDR-005 Account Lock State Transition Recorded)

analytics_modules: Cortex ITDR Analytics — Consecutive failure burst and lockout per principal
Cortex XSIAM Identity Analytics — Repeat-lockout password-spray grouping

products.evidence_query: dataset = msft_azure_ad_signin | filter auth_outcome = "FAILURE" | comp count() as c by user_name | filter c >= 10
Correlation CR-ITDR-0005 fuses the brute-force failure analytic and the lockout state-transition BIOC into one password-spray incident per identity.

use_cases.name: Account-Lockout State Transition
use_cases.description: Detect a consecutive-failure brute-force burst that locks an identity, capture the lockout transition, and escalate when repeat lockouts on the same target indicate password spray.

test_cases.objective: Ten consecutive failures lock the account and raise a brute-force anomaly.
preconditions: Cortex ITDR ingesting msft_azure_ad_signin including lockout result types.
idp_signin_emulator authorized against the lab collector.
steps: Observe the brute-force anomaly and lockout transition in the Cortex console.
success_criteria: XQL `ITDR-005 Ten Consecutive Failed Sign-Ins Brute Force` returns >= 1 row.
BIOC `ITDR-005 Account Lock State Transition Recorded` fires.

test_cases.objective: Repeat lockouts on one identity stitch into one password-spray incident.
success_criteria: Correlation `CR-ITDR-0005` groups the brute-force burst and lockout under one incident.

preventive_controls: Enforce smart lockout with progressive backoff and per-source throttling on the IdP.
Require phishing-resistant MFA so a guessed password alone never yields a session.
Block legacy/basic authentication endpoints that bypass conditional access.
detection_engineering: Count consecutive authentication FAILUREs per principal/source and alert at the lockout threshold.
Capture the lockout state-transition event and escalate on repeat lockouts within an hour.
Correlate rotating source IPs against one target to distinguish spray from a single brute-force origin.

references.title: Microsoft — Midnight Blizzard Password Spray Campaigns
